About Scitriad

What is Scitriad?

Scitriad is a free, interactive learning platform built for Alberta high school students. It turns complex topics in Science, Mathematics, and Humanities into engaging games — from tile-matching activities to physics simulators and debate strategy games.

Who is Scitriad for?

Scitriad is designed for Alberta high school students pursuing academic excellence, but anyone with curiosity is welcome. Whether you're reviewing for an exam, exploring a subject for the first time, or just looking for a fun way to learn, Scitriad has something for you.

Do I need an account to play?

No account is required to play any game. However, signing up lets Scitriad save your results so you can track your improvement over time and pick up where you left off across devices.

What subjects and topics are covered?

Scitriad currently covers Chemistry, Biology, Physics, Mathematics, and English. New games are added regularly across all subjects and difficulty levels.

How do the tile games work?

Tile games present a classification grid with empty slots. You select a property tile from the bank and place it into the correct cell. You can customise each session with settings like a timer, tile reveal mode, and a mistakes limit — accessible from the game settings popup before you start.

Scitriad | Free Interactive Learning Games
